properties.remark: AFTER COMPLETING A STORM SURVEY...A BRIEF TORNADO WAS CONFIRMED THROUGH PHOTOS OF VISIBILE DEBRIS. THIS TORNADO MAINLY MOVED OVER SAGEBRUSH AND NO VISBILE DAMAGE WAS FOUND. THIS TORNADO WAS ESTIMATED AS AN EF0.
MULTIPLE REPORTS FROM THE POST OFFICE AND CAR WASH OF QUARTER SIZE HAIL. LASTED LESS THAN 5 MINUTES.
TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER SUBMITTED VIDEO OF A ROTATING WALL CLOUD...WHICH HAS BEEN VERIFIED AS AN EF0 TORNADO AFTER A STORM SURVEY AND EYE WITNESS ACCOUNTS.
TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTS ROTATING WALL CLOUD...VERIFIED THROUGH VIDEO. ALSO SHINGLES AND ROOFING DEBRIS WERE REMOVED FROM A STRUCTURE. SMALL HAIL WAS ALSO REPORTED.
EF0 TORNADO WAS CONFIRMED THROUGH MULTIPLE PICTURES FROM MEDIA.A STORM SURVEY WAS COMPLETED...BUT NO MEASURABLE DAMAGE WAS FOUND. WINDS ESTIMATED AT 70 TO 80 MPH.
DRY MICROBURST AT AUSTIN RAWS...NO LIGHTNING, PROBABLY NO RAIN EITHER

DRY MICROBURST OCCURRED BETWEEN 600 AND 630 PM BASED OFF TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORT AND RADAR. VERY LITTLE...IF ANY...RAIN OR LIGHTNING OCCURRED WITH THIS STORM. SPOTTER REPORTED ONE HAY BARN DESTROYED AND ONE LARGE TREE UPROOTED.
DRY MICROBURST AT DESATOYA MOUNTAIN RAWS...NO LIGHTNING, PROBABLY NO RAIN EITHER
DRY MICROBURST AT EUREKA AIRPORT ASOS...NO LIGHTNING, PROBABLY NO RAIN REACHED THE GROUND.

DRY MICROBURST OCCURRED BETWEEN 600 AND 630 PM BASED OFF TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORT AND RADAR. VERY LITTLE...IF ANY...RAIN OR LIGHTNING OCCURRED WITH THIS STORM. SPOTTER REPORTED ONE HAY BARN DESTROYED.
DRY MICROBURST - RUBY LAKE RAWS.
DRY MICROBURST - ALLIGATOR RIDGE RAWS.
DRY MICROBURST AT THE COMBS CANYON RAWS STATION.
DRY MICROBURST AT THE ALLIGATOR RIDGE RAWS SITE.
PUBLIC REPORTED ON SOCIAL MEDIA A HIGHWAY SIGN WAS BLOWN OVER IN WEST WENDOVER. A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED SMALL BRANCHES DOWN AND ESTIMATED AT LEAST 50 MPH WINDS.
FLASH FLOODING WITH WATER AND DEBRIS ON THE ROAD. NDOT CLEARING THE ROAD.
NDOT REPORTS FLASH FLOODING FROM MILE MARKER 7 TO 14 ON STATE ROUTE 376. WATER AND DEBRIS FLOWING ACROSS ROAD.
NDOT REPORTS FLASH FLOODING FROM MILE MARKER 7 TO 14 ON STATE ROUTE 376. WATER AND DEBRIS FLOWING ACROSS ROAD. THE SHOULDERS WERE DAMAGED.
LOCAL LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTS FLASH FLOODING IN THE TOWN OF TONOPAH.

TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTERS REPORTED SIGNIFICANT FLASH FLOODING. WATER WAS ESTIMATED...AT ITS PEAK...OF 1 TO 1.5 FEET DEEP FLOWING THROUGH MAIN STREET. MANHOLE COVERS WERE BLOWN OFF AND OVERFLOWING. ROCKS AND DEBRIS WERE ON STREETS. ASPHALT WAS LIFTED FROM A SHELL GAS STATION. FLOOD WATERS DID CAUSE MINOR FLOODING IN ONE BUSINESS.

TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTERS REPORTED SIGNIFICANT FLASH FLOODING. WATER WAS ESTIMATED...AT ITS PEAK...OF 1 TO 1.5 FEET DEEP FLOWING THROUGH MAIN STREET. MANHOLE COVERS WERE OVERFLOWING AND ROCKS AND DEBRIS ARE IN STREETS.
1 FOOT DIAMETER TREE BRANCH FELL ON HOUSE
SHINGLES BLOWN OFF ROOF OF SENIOR CENTER
STREET FLOODING ON VARIOUS STREETS IN AND AROUND DOWNTOWN ELKO. AT LEAST 1/2 INCH OF RAIN FELL IN 15-20 MINUTES.
COOP OBSERVER REPORTED TREES DOWN IN TOWN, INCLUDING ONE ON A RANCH HOME.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL REPORTED FROM UNIVERSITY OF NEVADA-RENO GUND RANCH, 49 MILES SOUTH OF BEOWAWE. TIME APPROXIMATE.
WARM SPRINGS SUMMIT COMMUNITY ENVIRONMENTAL MONITORING PROGRAM MESONET SITE...7550 FEET ABOVE SEA LEVE
ELKO DAILY NEWSPAPER RELAYED REPORT OF FLASH FLOODING ON ELBURZ ROAD. CALLED AND SPOKE WITH SPOTTER. 3 FEET OF WATER FLOWING ACROSS THE ROAD WITH DEBRIS. ROAD DAMAGED AND FENCE DESTROYED.
HAIL COVERED THE GROUND AND WAS FOUND AROUND ONE TO ONE AND ONE HALF HOURS LATER.
NDOT REPORTED A MUD SLIDE ACROSS THE ROAD AT CHICKEN CREEK SUMMIT. NDOT SENDING EQUIPMENT TO CLEAR THE ROAD.
WATER AND MUD SEVERAL FEET DEEP ALONG THE SHOULDER OF U.S. ROUTE 93. TIME ESTIMATED.
WATER AND MUD SEVERAL FEET DEEP ALONG THE SHOULDER OF U.S. ROUTE 93. TIME ESTIMATED. NWS SURVEY COMPLETED.
TORNADO CONFIRMED THROUGH NWS AND BARRICK GOLDSTRIKE MINE SURVEY. THE TORNADO MOVED FROM WEST TO EAST FOR ONE HALF MILE...WITH A WIDTH OF 20 FEET WIDE. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED UP TO 80 MPH.
PUBLIC REPORTED TORNADO NEAR THE GOLDSTRIKE MINE IN NORTHERN EUREKA COUNTY. THIS WAS A LANDSPOUT TORNADO VERIFIED THROUGH PHOTOS.
FLOODING ALONG RURAL ROADWAYS WITH DEBRIS FLOW - NWS STORM SURVEY COMPLETED
COUNTY ROAD 728 HAD SHOULDER DAMAGE AND DEBRIS/MUD OVER THE ROAD. TIME ESTIMATED.

PIECE OF METAL ROOF WAS TORN OFF A SHED AND DAMAGE TO A SATELLITE RECIEVER WAS ALSO NOTED. THE METAL SHED DOORS WERE ALSO BLOWN IN. ESTIMATED WINDS OF 60 TO 70 MPH FROM A MICROBURST. NWS STORM SURVEY CONDUCTED.
ROCK SPRING CREEK RAWS. ELEVATION 5427 FT AMSL
WATER OVER RURAL ROADWAYS - NWS STORM SURVEY COMPLETED
WATER AND MUD ON STATE ROUTE 278
WATER ON U.S. ROUTE 50 THROUGH DEVILS GATE
EUREKA COUNTY SHERIFF REPORTED STANDING WATER ON DUCKWATER ROAD.
REPORT RELAYED THROUGH SOCIAL MEDIA OF MAIN STREET IN TONOPAH SHUT DOWN FROM FLASH FLOODING AND FLOOD DEBRIS.

FLASH FLOOD AND DEBRIS FLOW ON LEE ROAD JUST OFF STATE ROUTE 228. OFFICER ESTIMATED HALF MILE WIDE AREA OF WATER...ROCK...DEBRIS FLOWING ACROS THE ROAD 1 TO 3 FEET DEEP. EQUIPMENT WILL BE NEEDED TO REMOVE DEBRIS.

FLASH FLOOD AND DEBRIS FLOW ON LEE ROAD JUST OFF STATE ROUTE 228. OFFICER ESTIMATED HALF MILE WIDE AREA OF WATER...ROCK...DEBRIS FLOWING ACROS THE ROAD 1 TO 3 FEET DEEP. EQUIPMENT WILL BE NEEDED TO REMOVE DEBRIS. SHOULDER AND MINOR ROAD DAMAGE. TREES WERE CARRIED ACROSS THE ROAD AND DEPOSITED IN A DRY WASH BELOW THE ROAD.
PUBLIC REPORTED ONE FOOT OF WATER FLOWING ACROSS A ROAD NEAR SOUTH FORK. HAD TO MATCH WITH RADAR AND PRECIPITATION ESTIMATES.
MULTIPLE AREAS OF FLASH FLOODING ALONG COUTNY ROADS NORTH OF DEETH. SOME PLACES 1 TO 2 FEET DEEP...ROAD IMPASSABLE.
TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED 3 TO 4 INCHES OF FLOWING WATER ACROSS COUNTY ROADS NORTHEAST OF RYNDON.
PUBLIC REPORTED AT LEAST 6 INCHES OF WATER ON INTERSTATE 80 NEAR THE CARLIN TUNNELS. COVERING ONE THIRD OF THE INTERSTATE.
DRY WASH FLASH FLOODING AND FLOODED ROADWAYS. NO PROPERTY DAMAGE REPORTED.
FLASH FLOODING REPORTED IN AND AROUND RYNDON FROM HEAVY RAIN.
FLASH FLOODING FROM THE ELKO HILLS. WATER ACROSS ROADS AND RESIDENTAL PROPERTIES FLOODED. DEBRIS ALSO ON MANY ROADS ALONG BULLION ROAD AND ERRECART ROAD. NWS STORM SURVEY CONDUCTED.
6 INCHES OF WATER FLOWING DOWN THE SHOULDERS AND SIDE ROADS IN TONOPAH. ESTIMATED THREE FEET OF WATER IN DRY WASH FLASH FLOODING IN TONOPAH.
6 INCHES OF WATER FLOWING DOWN THE SHOULDERS AND SIDE ROADS IN TONOPAH. ESTIMATED THREE FEET OF WATER IN DRY WASH FLASH FLOODING IN TONOPAH.

NDOT REPORTS SIGNIFICANT FLASH FLOODING IN WILLOW CREEK CANYON FROM WALL TO WALL. ALSO INDIAN CREEK AND SNOWBALL CREEK HAD REPORTS OF FLASH FLOODING. DAMAGE TO THE ROADS WERE ALSO NOTED AROUND RANCHES IN THE VALLEY.
NDOT REPORTED FLASH FLOODING ON DUCKWATER ROAD IN WHITE PINE COUNTY NEAR POGUES STATION. TIME ESTIMATED BASED OFF RADAR.
REPORT RELAYED THROUGH SOCIAL MEDIA OF MAIN STREET IN TONOPAH SHUT DOWN FROM FLASH FLOODING AND FLOOD DEBRIS.
TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTERS ESTIMATED QUARTER SIZE HAIL...WHICH DAMAGED ALFALFA CROPS IN DIAMIOND VALLEY. OTHER NUMROUS REPORTS OF HAIL WERE OBSERVED ACROSS THE VALLEY.
SPOTTER REPORTED FLASH FLOODING WITH WATER ESTIMATED AT 4 INCHES DEEP FLOWING ACROSS ROADS AROUND DIAMOND VALLEY IN THE HIGHER TERRAIN. MINOR ROAD DAMAGE WAS ALSO REPORTED.
HAIL WAS ESTIMATED AT QUARTER SIZE BASED OFF PHOTOS. HAIL WAS FOUND WELL AFTER THE STORM PASSED.
NUMEROUS LOCATIONS OF WATER ACROSS THE ROADS IN NORTHWESTERN WHITE PINE COUNTY.
59 MPH GUST AT THE PILOT PEAK JUNCTION NDOT STATION.

NUMEROUS ROADS SHOWED EVIDENCE OF FLASH FLOODING WITH DAMAGE TO SOME OF THE ROADS ALONG THE GOSHUTE MOUNTAINS. FLOODING WAS ALSO EVIDENT IN PORTIONS OF NORTHERN WHITE PINE COUNTY. DRY WASHES ALSO WASHED ROCKS AND MUD ONTO SOME OF THE ROADS.

FLASH FLOODING AND NUMEROUS DEBRIS FLOWS WERE OBSERVED IN THE NWS STORM SURVEY. A TWO MILE STRETCH ON ALTERNATE STATE ROUTE 93 WAS FLOODED AND WAHSED A CAR OFF THE ROAD. FIRE AND RESCUE WERE DISPATCHED TO THE SCENE AND POSSIBLY INJURIES WERE REPORTED...BUT CANNOT BE VERIFIED. PARTIAL WASHOUTS ON DIRT ROADS WERE ALSO EVIDENT AND SAGE BRUSH WAS RIPPED FROM THE GROUND.
PUBLIC REPORTED AND WAS VERIFIED THROUCH A PHOTO OF A FENCE DESTROYED IN CARLIN.

WATER RAN ACROSS HIGHWAY 93 NEAR LAGES JUNCTION. DEBRIS WAS OBSERVED IN THE MIDDLE OF THE ROADWAY. MULTIPLE AREAS OF FLOODING WAS ALSO OBSERVED NEAR THE JUNCTION AS WELL AS THE SLOPES OF THE BURN SCAR.
SEVERAL REPORTS OF 1 TO 1.25 INCH HAIL IN ELKO. PICTURES WERE SENT THAT VERIFIED THE SIZE OF THE HAIL.
SEVERAL REPORTS OF 1 TO 1.25 INCH HAIL IN ELKO. PICTURES WERE SENT THAT VERIFIED THE SIZE OF THE HAIL. NWS EMPLOYEE ALSO REPORTED TREE DAMAGE AND DENTS TO VEHICLES ALONG MOUNTAIN CITY HIGHWAY.
FLASH FLOODING AND DEBRIS ACROSS MUSTANG ROAD IN THE DIAMOND VALLEY AREA.
FLASH FLOODING WITH DEBRIS OVER THR ROADWAY. FLOODING OCCURRED BETWEEN MILE MARKER 24 AND 26 ON STATE ROUTE 892 IN THE NEWARK VALLEY.
STATE ROUTE 278 BETWEEN MILE MARKERS 14 AND 17...FLASH FLOODING WITH DEBRIS AND WATER ACROSS THE ROAD. PLOW TRUCK NEEDED TO CLEAR DEBRIS AND WATER OFF THE ROAD.
NDOT REPORTED WATER OVER THE ROADWAY WITH A LOT OF DEBRIS. THIS WAS ON STATE ROUTE 893 AT MILE MARKER 40.

SEVERAL DEBRIS FLOWS DUE TO FLASH FLOODING OVER HAMPTON, HORSE, AND SMITH CREEKS. VERIFIED BY BURNED AREA EMERGENCY RESPONSE HYDROLOGIST AND THROUGH PHOTOS. DEBRIS FLOW ESTIMATED DEPTH OF 6-8 FEET IN THE CANYON, 1-2 FEET IN THE BASIN.
TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED QUARTER SIZE HAIL AND STRONG GUSTY WINDS.
HAIL SLIGHTLY LARGER THAN PEA SIZE AND STRONG WINDS TOOK OFF A FEW SHINGLES.

HAIL SIZE VERIFIED THROUGH PHOTOS. LOCATION WAS ALONG COAL MINE CANYON ROAD AND TIME WAS ESTIMATED BASED OFF RADAR. RESIDENTS VEHICLE WAS ALSO DENTED FROM THE HAIL AND STATED THAT HAIL ACCUMULATION WAS AT LEAST ONE FOOT DEEP IN PORTIONS OF HIS YARD.
PUBLIC REPORTED HAIL SIZES FROM DIMES TO LARGER THAN QUARTERS AT GOLD QUARRY MINE.
PUBLIC REPORTED HAIL SIZES FROM DIMES TO LARGER THAN QUARTERS AT GOLD QUARRY MINE. ANOTHER REPORT CAME IN OF DENTED VEHICLES AND HAIL BUSTED OUT AT LEAST ONE WINDOW AT THE MINE.
THIS WAS VERIFIED THROUGH PHOTOS.
QUARTER INCH HAIL REPORTED BY NWS EMPLOYEE AND CONFIRMED BY PHOTO. ALSO SIGNIFICANT HAIL DAMAGE DONE TO VEHICLES PARKED AT LAMOILLE CANYON.
PUBLIC REPORTED FLASH FLOODING ON BOYD-KENNEDY ROAD.
STAG MOUNTAIN RAWS AT AN ELEVATION OF 6790 FT.
ELBURZ ROAD WAS WASHED OUT DUE TO FLASH FLOODING.

A CULVERT FLOODED AND DAMAGED A ROAD WELL AFTER THUNDERSTORM RAINS HAD ENDED. COAL MINE CANYON ROAD HAD TO BE CLOSED THE NEXT DAY TO REPAIR NUMEROUS WASHOUTS AND ROAD DAMAGE. A NWS STORM SURVEY WAS CONDUCTED AND THROUGOUT RYNDON MINOR LOWLAND FLOODING AND PONDING WAS OBSERVED.
REPORTS OF DEBRIS FROM FLOODING ALL OVER PARK. PARK IS A MESS.
DEBRIS FLOW CAUSED BY FLASH FLOODING OCCURRED OVER THE HAMPTON BURN SCAR AREA. ESTIMATED DEPTH OF DEBRIS 6-12 INCHES. CONFIRMED BY NATIONAL PARK SERVICE EMPLOYEE.
FLASH FLOOD WITH DEBRIS FLOW CROSSED US93-ALTERNATE WITH UP TO A HALF FOOT OF WATER/DEBRIS OVER ROADWAY. CONFIRMED THROUGH VIDEO SENT BY PUBLIC.
FLASH FLOOD WITH DEBRIS FLOW CROSSED US93 WITH UP TO A HALF FOOT OF WATER/DEBRIS OVER ROADWAY. CONFIRMED THROUGH VIDEO SENT BY PUBLIC.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL THAT DAMAGED TREES.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL WAS REPORTED AND COVERED THE GROUND.
WATER AND DEBRIS OVER STATE ROUTE 376 BETWEEN MP 8-14 OCCURRED BETWEEN NOON AND 10 PM.
WATER AND DEBRIS OVER US HWY 6 MP 54-58 BETWEEN 4 AND 9 PM.
WATER AND DEBRIS OVER STATE ROUTE 318 AT MP 23 AT 9PM. CAUSED BY DEBRIS COVERING CULVERT OPENING ALLOWING WATER AND DEBRIS TO GO OVER THE ROAD.
THUNDERSTORM PRODUCED 66 MPH GUST AT ROCK SPRING CREEK RAWS

PUBLIC REPORTED VIA FACEBOOK...HEAVY RAIN AND 1.00 INCH HAIL AT THEIR LOCATION. PREVIOUS REPORT INDICATING TIME OF 5 PM AND LOCATION 8 MILES SOUTH WAS AN ESTIMATE BEFORE ADDITIONAL INFORMATION MADE AVAILABLE.
PUBLIC REPORTED VIA FACEBOOK...HEAVY RAIN AND 1.00 INCH HAIL AT THEIR LOCATION.
THUNDERSTORM PRODUCED 66 MPH GUST AT ROCK SPRING CREEK RAWS

AN EF-0 TORNADO WAS RELAYED THROUGH A STORM SPOTTER AND WAS VERIFIED THROUGH PICTURES. THIS TORNADO OCCURRED IN THE MOUNTAINS AND NO STORM SURVEY WILL BE CONDUCTED. TORNADO WAS IN THE BUTLER BASIN AND BUTLER CREEK AREA.

FLASH FLOODING WAS RELAYED THROUGH NDOT...WATER AND DEBRIS ACROSS THE ROADWAY. WATER APPARENTLY CAME DOWN FROM AN OLD BURN SCAR AND HAD A LAG TIME BEFORE IMPACTING THE ROAD. A RAIN GUAGE IN THE VICINITY RECORDED 1.11 INCHES OF RAIN. TIME WAS ESTIMATED FROM NDOT AND RADAR.

NDOT SHARED A VIDEO OF FLASH FLOODING ON A COUNTY ROAD ON THE WEST SIDE OF THE DIAMOND RANGE. TIME WAS ESTIMATED OFF NDOT/RADAR. 20FT SECTION OF CULVERT WAS WASHED AWAY AND CANNOT BE LOCATED. THE ROAD WAS DAMAGED.
WATER OVER US ROUTES 6 AND 50 NEAR SACRAMENTO PASS
WATER OVER US ROUTES 6 AND 50 NEAR SACRAMENTO PASS. 10 INCHES OF FLOWING WATER WAS ALSO REPORTED ACROSS HIGHWAY 6 ON THE WESTERN SIDE OF SACRAMENTO PASS.
WATER FLOWING ACROSS STATE ROUTE 278 AT MILE MARKER 32
WATER, ROCKS AND MUD ON BELMONT ROAD. EXACT LOCATION UNCERTAIN.
PUBLIC REPORTED FLASH FLOOD ON IBAPAH ROAD AT UTAH/NEVADA BORDER VIA FACEBOOK.
TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED THAT ONE FOOT OF WATER WAS FLOWING OVER HIS BRIDGE. THIS REPORTS WAS FROM TAYLOR CANYON ALONG STATE ROUTE 226.
TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED THAT ONE FOOT OF WATER WAS FLOWING OVER HIS BRIDGE. THIS REPORTS WAS FROM TAYLOR CANYON ALONG STATE ROUTE 226. EDIT-THIS QUALIFIES AS MINOR FLOODING.

EDIT FROM PREVIOUS REPORT LISTED AT 515 PM TIME CORRECTED TO 450 PM-TIME OF WORST FLOODING. SEVERE FLOODING ALONG STATE ROUTE 226 IN TAYLOR CANYON. BOULDERS 2-3 FEET IN DIAMETER ON ROAD WITH WATER UP TO 5 FEET DEEP OVER THE ROAD. SMALL HAIL DRIFTS SEVERAL FEET DEEP ALONG SIDES OF ROADWAY. SAFETY PERSONNEL ALSO REPORT FIVE CABINS, SIX CAMP TRAILERS, A SINGLEWIDE PARTIALLY SUBMERGED. MULTIPLE CARS SUBMERGED AND SEVERAL MOVED BY WATER. ONE SMALL HOUSE AND AT LEAST ONE CABIN DISLODGED FROM FOUNDATIO

SEVERE FLOODING SR 226/TAYLOR CANYON. BOULDERS 2-3 FEET ON ROAD. WATER UP TO 5 FEET DEEP ON ROAD. SMALL HAIL DRIFTS SEVERAL FEET DEEP ALONG SIDES OF ROAD. SEVERAL CABINS, SIX CAMP TRAILERS AND A SINGLEWIDE PARTIALLY SUBMERGED. MULTIPLE CARS SUBMERGED AND SEVERAL MOVED BY WATER. AT LEAST ONE SWIFT WATER RESCUE. LOCAL BUSINESS, GARAGE AND HOMES INUNDATED WITH SEVERAL FEET OF MUD AND WATER.
SEVERE FLOODING ALONG STATE ROUTE 226 IN TAYLOR CANYON. BOULDERS ON ROAD AND POSSIBLY PARTIAL ROAD WASHOUT.

SEVERE FLOODING ALONG STATE ROUTE 226 IN TAYLOR CANYON. BOULDERS ON ROAD AND POSSIBLY PARTIAL ROAD WASHOUT. SAFETY PERSONNEL ALSO REPORT FIVE CABINS, SIX CAMP TRAILERS, A SINGLEWIDE, AND A NUMBER OF CARS WERE DAMAGED AS WELL.
2 FEET OF WATER FLOWING ACROSS STATE ROUTE 278 SOUTH OF CARLIN.

DUST WAS LOFTED FROM DIAMOND VALLEY AND MOVED NORTH INTO THE HUMBOLDT RIVER VALLEY AND SPRING CREEK. ELKO AP ASOS REPORTED VISIBILITY DOWN TO 1.25 MILES. LOCALIZED VISIBILITY DROPPED BELOW ONE MILE AND A SPOTTER HAD TO PULL OF THE ROAD TO WAIT FOR THE DUST TO PASS. THE DUST STORM OCCURED BETWEEN 11AM AND 2PM.
